branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Depot & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Marlins pride

columnists.name: Horatio Ruiz
columnists.backstory: A Little Havana reporter who covered both Marlins World Series runs and never once let the empty seats change his column.
columnists.signature_phrase: The ledger, en blanco y negro.
columnists.voice_sample: Arráez went 3-for-4, Alcántara pitched into the seventh, and Miami beat the Braves 4-1 at loanDepot park.

columnists.name: Lucinda 'Lucy' Pérez-Alvarado
columnists.backstory: A Coral Gables dental hygienist who believes the Marlins are due for their third fire-sale dynasty and nobody can tell her otherwise.
columnists.voice_sample: So what if the stadium's empty? That's more room for the echo of Arráez's next 2-strike line drive. This team is a storm gathering.

columnists.name: Tito Escalante
columnists.backstory: A Hialeah retired bus driver who was at Pro Player for Game 7 in '97 and thinks every Marlins ownership group is personally trying to insult him.
columnists.voice_sample: Bruce Sherman bought a ball team and a museum of unsold tickets. Every spring is a fresh betrayal. I'm out of patience and I'm out of beer.
